title: In-Context Model Predictive Generation: Open-Vocabulary Motion Synthesis from Language Models to Physics
abstract: arXiv:2606.26981v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Synthesizing human motion from textual descriptions is essential for immersive digital applications, yet existing methods face a persistent trade-off between semantic fidelity and physical realism. Large language model (LLM)-based approaches can interpret diverse open-vocabulary instructions and compose high-level action plans, but they often generate motions that violate physical constraints. Physics-aware models improve realism through simulation or control, but they struggle with semantic complexity, fine-grained instructions, and novel concepts. To address this gap, we propose In-Context Model Predictive Generation (ICMPG), a framework that integrates language-model planning with inference-time physical feedback. ICMPG reformulates motion synthesis as a Model Predictive Control (MPC)-like process with two modules. The Context-Aware Motion Generation (CAMG) module uses an LLM as a planner to decompose textual commands and generate c

Abstract: arXiv:2606.26981v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Synthesizing human motion from textual descriptions is essential for immersive digital applications, yet existing methods face a persistent trade-off between semantic fidelity and physical realism. Large language model (LLM)-based approaches can interpret diverse open-vocabulary instructions and compose high-level action plans, but they often generate motions that violate physical constraints. Physics-aware models improve realism through simulation or control, but they struggle with semantic complexity, fine-grained instructions, and novel concepts. To address this gap, we propose In-Context Model Predictive Generation (ICMPG), a framework that integrates language-model planning with inference-time physical feedback. ICMPG reformulates motion synthesis as a Model Predictive Control (MPC)-like process with two modules. translated_title: 上下文模型预测生成：从语言模型到物理的开放词汇运动合成
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26981v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：从文本描述合成人体运动对于沉浸式数字应用至关重要，然而现有方法在语义保真度和物理真实性之间面临持续的权衡。基于大语言模型（LLM）的方法能够解释多样的开放词汇指令并组合高级行动计划，但它们生成的运动常常违反物理约束。物理感知模型通过模拟或控制提高了真实性，但在处理语义复杂性、细粒度指令和新概念时存在困难。为弥补这一差距，我们提出了上下文模型预测生成（ICMPG），这是一个将语言模型规划与推理时物理反馈相结合的框架。ICMPG将运动合成重新构架为一种类似模型预测控制（MPC）的过程，包含两个模块。上下文感知运动生成（CAMG）模块使用LLM作为规划器来分解文本命令并生成c
tldr: 本文提出ICMPG框架，通过将大语言模型的规划能力与推理时的物理反馈相结合，解决了从文本合成人体运动时语义保真度与物理真实性之间的权衡问题。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes the ICMPG framework, which addresses the trade-off between semantic fidelity and physical realism in text-to-human-motion synthesis by integrating large language model planning with inference-time physics feedback.
